SPEC INACCURACY (§Profile Registry / Defaults): spec states DataModeProvider.tsx is co-located in `shared/infrastructure/connectivity/` (`DataModeProvider.tsx:58–63`). REALITY: resolveDataMode/DataModeProvider live at museum-frontend/features/chat/application/DataModeProvider.tsx (NOT shared/infrastructure/connectivity/). isOnline.ts IS in shared/infrastructure/connectivity/. The registry will be authored in shared/infrastructure/connectivity/networkProfiles.ts (co-located with isOnline.ts) and the self-test imports resolveDataMode from `@/features/chat/application/DataModeProvider`. Line numbers 44/52/56/58 are EXACT.

SPEC MODEL CHOICE (§Profile Registry / Security): spec says model the parity sentinel on `cache-key-parity.mjs` (same doctrine). REALITY: scripts/sentinels/cache-key-parity.mjs is NOT a structural parity sentinel — it is a thin spawnSync wrapper that runs a jest test (tests/contract/cache-key-parity.test.ts). The correct model for a pure sha256 byte-comparison sentinel is the EXISTING scripts/sentinels/sentry-scrubber-parity.mjs (uses node:crypto createHash) combined with the pure-structural string-check style of museum-backend/scripts/sentinels/security-headers-invariants.mjs. Plan uses these instead.

SPEC self-test netInfo shape (§Self-test invariant) lists `{isConnected, type, details.cellularGeneration, isConnectionExpensive, isConnectionExpensive}` — the REAL resolveDataMode reads `isConnectionExpensive` from `netInfo.details.isConnectionExpensive` (line 68), NOT a top-level field. The self-test snapshot must nest isConnectionExpensive under details (confirmed by DataModeProvider.test.tsx).

SPEC WRONG: httpClient.ts:168 axios timeout — REAL: the timeout:15000 property is at httpClient.ts:172; line 168 is the `axios.create({` opening. Use AXIOS_TIMEOUT_MS=15000 sourced from httpClient.ts:172.

SPEC MISLEADING (M5): spec says timeout-budget binding uses '[500,2000,8000] schedule, 15s axios → Timeout' as if one mechanism. REALITY: [500,2000,8000] = DEFAULT_BACKOFF_MS in retry.ts:34 consumed by runWithRetry/useOfflineSync; the AXIOS interceptor (httpClient.ts:314–346) uses a DIFFERENT schedule (150*(retryCount+1) for non-429, exponential 1s/2s/4s for 429, max 2 retries, isRetryable computed inline at :318–319). M5 must test runWithRetry against the [500,2000,8000] schedule, NOT the axios interceptor.

SPEC OPTIMISTIC (M4/D2): spec implies Idempotency-Key forwarding is a small addition at useOfflineSync.ts:60. REALITY: it does not exist at all and requires a 3-file change — PostMessageParams (send.ts:20–37) has no key field; postMessage (send.ts:81) only sets X-Data-Mode; useOfflineSync peek() return type (useOfflineSync.ts:21) and useOfflineQueue.peek (useOfflineQueue.ts:72) both OMIT id, so queued.id is not even reachable at the flush call (useOfflineSync.ts:59–69). M4 red test legitimately fails today.

SPEC GAP: spec says helpers live in shared/testing/ — that directory does NOT exist yet (net-new). Same for mulberry32/FakeClock (none in repo). makeQueuedMessage factory does NOT exist (net-new, DRY-required).
SPEC GAP: no no-restricted-imports rule exists in eslint.config.mjs today — the shared/testing/** boundary is net-new, not a tweak.

Spec §244 says the picker 'reads useDataMode()' — CORRECT hook exists at DataModeProvider.tsx:101, but spec implies a trivial wiring; reality: useCompareImagePicker derives MIME from the URI EXTENSION (describeImage→normalizeImageMimeTypeFromExtension), so emitting WebP REQUIRES the optimized URI to end in .webp. manipulateAsync(format:WEBP) does produce a .webp file, so the chain works, but this dependency is not called out in the spec and is load-bearing.

Spec §238 proposes signature optimizeImageForUpload(uri, profile=IMAGE_PROFILES.normal) returning backwards-compatibly — but the function currently returns a string and existing frozen tests assert a string return. The two-output (D1) requirement cannot be expressed by the same string-returning signature; plan adds a sibling adaptive function / overload rather than changing the legacy return type, to keep the 9 frozen tests green.

Spec ledger lists imageUploadOptimization.ts:6-8 as VERIFIED — confirmed accurate. SaveFormat.WEBP existence is also confirmed statically (spec §66 left it as a runtime check; static support is real, only native-encoder fidelity remains a runtime check).

SPEC R9 ('Server image persistence may not exist yet') is FALSE — verified: ImageProcessingService.processImage persists the upload via imageStorage.save() (image-processing.service.ts:97), backed by S3 or local-stub adapters. No BE persistence work needed.

SPEC §(a-bis)/D4 ('an authenticated GET re-download path serves it ... if absent ... is added within W1') OVERSTATES the BE gap — verified: BOTH an authenticated re-download paths ALREADY exist (POST .../image-url ownership-checked at chat-media.route.ts:277, GET .../image signed-token at :294) and are in openapi.json. BE W1 = verify/lock the contract + ensure post-wipe uses fresh image-url (not stale signed GET replay), NOT build-from-scratch.

SPEC §(a-bis) ('offlineImageStorage.ts becomes a cache with a dedicated cap') correctly identifies the FE gap, but note the file TODAY has ZERO caching primitives (no index, no size accounting, no eviction) — it is a full rewrite-in-place of a 55-line persist helper, and its 3 exported fns are consumed by useOfflineQueue.ts:9-12 (run gitnexus_impact before editing to avoid breaking the offline-queue resync path).

SPEC anchor image-processing.service.ts:61 (cited in §(a)/D1 as 'backend accepts image/webp') points at the constructor (this.imageStorage = deps.imageStorage), NOT a webp-accept check; mime allow-list is env.upload.allowedMimeTypes asserted at image-processing.service.ts:89. Webp end-to-end acceptance is a D1 (compression cluster) concern, not D4.

Spec §82/§320 cites scripts/sentinels/cache-key-parity.mjs as the parity-sentinel template — it does NOT exist in this repo (verified: ls scripts/sentinels/ has no cache-key-parity.mjs). The real structural-sentinel template is scripts/sentinels/security-headers-invariants.mjs. W1-GZIP-08 models on that.

Spec §253 implies env.jsonBodyLimit is '1 MB' as a number — it is the STRING '1mb' (env.ts:66, typed string). The zip-bomb cap MUST parse that string to bytes (e.g. 1mb -> 1048576); cannot compare bytes against a raw string. Load-bearing for W1-GZIP-02/06.

Spec §253 places the cap discussion only relative to express.json (:177). Verified additional fact: the timeout setter (res.setTimeout, app.ts:172-175) sits BETWEEN compression (170) and express.json (177); the decompression middleware can mount anywhere after compression and before express.json — recommend mounting immediately before express.json (after the timeout setter) so the inflate stream still benefits from the request socket timeout.

pako is NOT yet a museum-frontend dependency (verified: node_modules/pako absent, not in package.json). W1-GZIP-07 must add it. There is NO lib-docs/pako/ — doc-cache must fetch pako docs in the red/green phase (libDocs:['pako'] will require a fresh cache entry).

Spec §(c) line 257 and D2 row imply FE 'forwards queued.id as Idempotency-Key (useOfflineSync.ts:60)' as if present/partial — FALSE. useOfflineSync.ts forwards NO idempotency key; line 60 is `chatApi.postMessage({` opening the call object. Forwarding is fully net-new.

Spec understates FE scope: it is NOT just a one-line header add. The hook's peek() accessor type (useOfflineSync.ts:21) DROPS the queue item id, so closing D2 also requires widening peek()/UseOfflineSyncParams to surface QueuedMessage.id and threading it from the caller that constructs peek.

Spec D2 says dedup store 'e.g. short-TTL Redis or in-memory with TTL' (undecided). DECIDED: Redis-via-CacheService primary with in-process fallback (mirrors guardrail-friction.store + the existing NoopCacheService fallback), because a pure in-memory Map would let a duplicate slip through a second worker; use CacheService.setNx (cache.port.ts:26) as the atomic reserve. Policy = FAIL-OPEN (store outage never 500s the chat nor blocks first send), matching guardrail-friction FAIL-SOFT doctrine.

Spec glossary line 46 + §'Force-low hook' imply `useDataModePreferenceStore.setPreference` and §81 puts the registry/connectivity under `shared/infrastructure/connectivity/`. CORRECTED: the data-mode preference store is NOT in connectivity — it lives at `museum-frontend/features/settings/dataModeStore.ts:30` (exported `useDataModePreferenceStore`, action `setPreference` at :35). DataModeProvider imports it from `@/features/settings/dataModeStore`. The force-data-mode route MUST import from `@/features/settings/dataModeStore`.

Spec line 288 says the route 'redirects' generically. CONFIRMED-EXACT: the canonical redirect target is `/` (root), matching `(dev)/_layout.tsx` `<Redirect href="/" />` and `app/index.tsx` redirect convention — use `router.replace('/')` or `<Redirect href="/">`.

Spec line 288 phrasing 'Route resets to 'auto' on unmount (or flows always clearState)'. CLARIFIED as a firm requirement (R6): implement the unmount reset (`setPreference('auto')`) IN the route via `useEffect` cleanup — do NOT rely on flows calling clearState (the route owns its leak-prevention; verified the store default is 'auto' so reset is correct).
